content.text: Contractor intake, form 1 of 1. Answer honestly. The file gets completed either way.
No classes, no levels. A contractor is assembled from parts — creation hands you the starting set, and mission pay buys everything after.
The Layers
Each layer of a character answers a different question. Three are picked at creation; the other two are what your career buys later.

rows: Bought in play — Trees Overview (your kit installs its tree's entry augment free)
Bought in play — Techniques (your kit includes one)

content.text: The Sequence
Pick a résumé. Two skills at Tier 1, a standing line, and your starting credits — see Résumés.
Pick your species. Verbs, economies, and paperwork — never stat numbers. See Playable Species.
Pick a trait. Exactly one, and every trait gives with one hand while the ledger takes with the other — see Traits.
Assign your stats. The array below, across Chrome, Reflex, Grit, Interface, Edge.
Pick a kit. The day-one package: gear loadout, one free tree entry augment, one free Trained technique — see Kits.
Compute the derived numbers. Evasion, Max Flesh HP, vent, and the rest — see Finishing.
Read your file. The corp opens a dossier on you the day you sign. Most of its fields are empty. That's the job: filling them in, one contract at a time.

content.label: THE ARRAY
content.text: Your five stats begin as 8 / 7 / 6 / 5 / 4, assigned freely across Chrome, Reflex, Grit, Interface, and Edge.

Point-buy variant: the same budget as numbers — 30 points, spent 1-for-1, minimum 3 in any stat, maximum 8 at creation. Eight is a peak-trained civilian; 9 and 10 are career purchases behind the elite access funnel, and the chrome band starts past that — see Stats & Growth.
Species never modify stats. Traits never modify stats. The array is the array; everything a species or trait does is verbs and economies.
Day One, In Total
Two skills at Tier 1, from your résumé — every other skill rolls untrained (The Two Rolls).
One trait, both sides of it.
A gear loadout, one tree entry augment, and one technique, from your kit.
A standing line and starting credits, from your résumé.
Everything after day one is bought: stat training on Stats & Growth, augments on Trees Overview, techniques on Techniques — and the long view of what a career costs sits on The Anchor Table.
Welcome aboard, contractor. Your starting package reflects the career your previous life prepared you for. Any gap between who you were and who you intend to become can be financed.
everything in this city can be financed. that's not the comfort they think it is.
